18 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Pentagon prepares 1,500 soldiers to deploy for Minnesota protests crackdown Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- The Pentagon has ordered some 1,500 active-duty soldiers to be prepared to deploy for crackdown on protests in Minnesota.

  • US DEA Labeled Venezuela's Rodriguez as 'Priority Target' in 2022 - Reports Sputnik 18 Jan 2026 -- The US Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) designated current Venezuelan Acting President Delcy Rodriguez as a priority target in 2022, the Associated Press reported citing documents and US law enforcement officials.
  • Venezuela has right to build relations with Iran, other countries: Delcy Rodriguez Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- Venezuela's interim president, Delcy Rodriguez, has reaffirmed that Caracas has the sovereign right to maintain diplomatic relations with China, Cuba, Iran, and Russia, rejecting US interference and denouncing Washington's aggressive posture toward the country.
  • UAE's state oil company weighs major investment in Venezuela's gas: Report Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates' state oil company, the Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (Adnoc), is considering a significant investment in Venezuela's natural gas industry, according to industry reports.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Statement by Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Norway, Sweden and the United Kingdom: Finnish Government 18 Jan 2026
  • Statement by President Costa on Greenland EC/CEU 18 Jan 2026
  • PM calls with international leaders: 18 January 2026 Gov.UK 18 Jan 2026
  • Trump says U.S. to impose tariffs on 8 European nations over Greenland Global Times 18 Jan 2026 -- The United States will impose 10-percent tariffs on all goods from Denmark, Norway, Sweden, France, Germany, Britain, the Netherlands and Finland over Greenland starting on Feb. 1, U.S. President Donald Trump said on Saturday.
  • EU mulls over $100bn in US retaliatory tariffs over Greenland Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- The European Union says it is preparing a major package of financial countermeasures against the US in response to Washington's threats against the bloc over Greenland.
  • EU leaders say they won't be 'blackmailed' by Trump's Greenland tariffs threat Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump's threat of sanctions against the European countries that reject any American claim to Greenland has prompted a coordinated response from European countries.
  • UK Stance on US Purchase of Greenland Remains 'Non-Negotiable' - Culture Secretary Sputnik 18 Jan 2026 -- Greenland and its citizens should themselves determine the future of the country, UK Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport Lisa Nandy said on Sunday, adding that the UK government's stance on this matter remains "non-negotiable."
  • Dutch Foreign Minister Calls Trump's Tariff Threats 'Blackmail' Sputnik 18 Jan 2026 -- Dutch Foreign Minister David van Weel on Sunday called US President Donald Trump's threats to introduce tariffs on several European countries over Greenland blackmail.
  • US House Speaker Johnson Believes Talks Best Way to Reach Progress on Greenland - Reports Sputnik 18 Jan 2026 -- US House Speaker Mike Johnson told the Times in an interview that he believes negotiations are the most efficient approach to resolving the ongoing situation regarding the United States' claim to Greenland.
  • Trump's Tariffs Over Greenland Trigger GOP Backlash: 'Bad for America & Allies' Sputnik 18 Jan 2026 -- Donald Trump's tariffs on eight European countries opposing his Greenland plans are "unnecessary, punitive, and a profound mistake," Alaska's Republican Senator Lisa Murkowski wrote on X.
  • Finland Calls Out Trump's Tariffs Over Greenland: 'This is a Power Play' Sputnik 18 Jan 2026 -- Finnish lawmakers are calling on Europe to shake off its reliance on the US after Donald Trump announced tariffs against eight European countries opposing his plan to seize control of Greenland, Iltalehti reports.
  • Trump's tariff threat over Greenland sparks European pushback; Chinese experts note widening transatlantic divide Global Times 18 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump's threat to impose new tariffs on eight European countries unless Denmark accepts a deal over Greenland has drawn swift rebukes from Nordic capitals and major European leaders, fueled calls for an EU-level response, and cast fresh doubt over the implementation of last year's EU-US tariff "truce," the Xinhua News Agency reported Sunday.

  • Trump Wants to Buy More Icebreakers to Patrol Off Canada - Reports Sputnik 18 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has expressed interest in purchasing additional icebreakers to patrol waters off Canada, NBC News reported on Sunday, citing US officials.

  • NATO Task Group conducts port visit to Riga following change of command NATO Allied Maritime Command 18 Jan 2026 -- Standing NATO Maritime Group One (SNMG1) has arrived in Riga, Latvia, marking the task group's first port visit since Spain assumed command of the force. The visit underscores NATO's enduring commitment to collective defence and maritime security in the Baltic Sea region.

  • Taiwan's new military police unit equipped with Stingers and Javelins CNA 18 Jan 2026 -- A newly formed company under the 202nd Military Police Command in Taipei has been equipped with United States-made FIM-92 Stinger shoulder-fired missiles and Javelin man-portable anti-tank systems to enhance the defense of the capital city, according to an anonymous source.

  • Govt is committed to achieving Aatmanirbharta in ammunition manufacturing & make the country a global production hub: Raksha Mantri India PIB 18 Jan 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh has voiced Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi-led Government's commitment to achieving Aatmanirbharta in ammunition manufacturing and make the country a global production hub in the field
  • INDIAN NAVY CONCLUDES JOINT SERVICES MULTI-SPECIALITY MEDICAL CAMP AT THE LAKSHADWEEP ISLANDS India PIB 18 Jan 2026 -- The Indian Navy successfully concluded the five-day Joint Services Multi-Speciality Medical Camp across the Lakshadweep Islands on 16 Jan 2026.
  • INS SAGARDHWANI FLAGS OFF FOR SAGAR MAITRI V India PIB 18 Jan 2026 -- INS Sagardhwani, India's oceanographic research vessel under the Naval Physical and Oceanographic Laboratory (NPOL) of DRDO, was flagged off for the fifth edition of the Sagar Maitri (SM-5) initiative from Southern Naval Command, Kochi on 17 Jan 2026.

  • The Minister of Defense of the Republic of Armenia received the Deputy Secretary General of NATO Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 18 Jan 2026 -- On December 18, the Minister of Defense of the Republic of Armenia Suren Papikyan received the Deputy Secretary General of NATO Ms. Radmila Shekerinska.

  • Joint maritime drill among BRICS members wraps up off South Africa; features practicing rescuing hijacked commercial vessel: media Global Times 18 Jan 2026 -- BRICS members, including China, Russia and South Africa, recently wrapped up a joint maritime exercise off South Africa, in which they practiced rescuing a hijacked commercial vessel, according to official Chinese media on Sunday.

  • One Year Since the Return of Fallen Hostage, Oron Shaul: The IDF and the ISA Reveal Operation "Red South" Which Was Carried Out to Bring Him Home Israel Defense Forces 18 Jan 2026
  • Multi-Dimensional Unit Troops Complete Their Mission in Southern Lebanon Israel Defense Forces 18 Jan 2026

  • Trump demands countries pay $1 bn to join Gaza 'Board of Peace': Report Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- The administration of US President Donald Trump is demanding that countries contribute at least $1 billion if they want a permanent position on his so-called Gaza Board of Peace, a report says.
  • Trump Wants Countries to Pay $1Bln for Permanent Seat in Gaza Board of Peace - Reports Sputnik 18 Jan 2026 -- The administration of US President Donald Trump is asking countries to pay over $1 billion for the right to a permanent seat on the Gaza Board of Peace, Bloomberg reported citing the board's draft charter.

  • Aboul Gheit welcomes formation of Palestinian technocratic committee to administer Gaza League of Arab States 18 Jan 2026
  • OIC General Secretariat Welcomes the Start of the Second Phase and the Formation of a Palestinian Committee to Administer the Gaza Strip, and Affirms theUunity of Palestinian Territory Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 18 Jan 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) welcomed the start of the second phase of the comprehensive peace plan under UN Security Council Resolution 2803, as well as the announcement of the formation of the Palestinian committee to administer the Gaza Strip as a temporary transitional body
  • Press Remarks by the Spokesperson Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 18 Jan 2026 -- In response to media queries, the Spokesperson of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Mr Tahir Andrabi, stated the following:

    • Other Conflicts

  • No: 9, 18 January 2026, Regarding the Ceasefire and Full Integration Agreement Announced in Syria Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 18 Jan 2026
  • Syria's HTS regime agrees to truce with SDF after its troops advance Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- The leader of Syria's Hay'at Tahrir al‑Sham administration has announced a deal with the head of the so-called Syrian Democratic Forces that includes a ceasefire, following HTS advances across SDF-held areas in the north and east.

  • Sudan is scene of 'horror and hell': UN rights chief Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- The United Nations rights chief has warned that the Sudanese people are trapped in a scene of "horror and hell."
  • UN rights chief bears witness to trauma and resilience in Sudan UN News 18 Jan 2026 -- Nearly three years of war have plunged Sudan "into an abyss of unimaginable dimensions" and human rights must be at the centre of efforts to end the fighting and build lasting peace, the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights said on Sunday in Nairobi.

News Reports

  • EU, South American countries ink trade deal sending 'clear signal' to Trump Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- The European Union and Mercosur blocs have signed an international trade pact, sending a "clear signal" to US President Donald Trump to stop threatening world countries with economic tariffs.

  • New Sanctions On Russian Oil Hit Indian, Turkish Refineries. Enter China? RFE/RL 18 Jan 2026 -- On a mild and cloudy evening in Izmit, western Turkey, a Panamanian-flagged tanker called the Bela 6 dropped anchor and began pumping nearly 100,000 tons of Russian oil.

  • US House speaker's claim over Chinese embassy plan in London 'a typical US politician's mindset of turning ally into expendable consumables': Chinese expert Global Times 18 Jan 2026 -- Following Washington's blatant interference in UK's internal affairs a few days earlier, Mike Johnson, the Speaker of the US House of Representatives, has reportedly claimed during his visit to the UK that he personally is concerned about the Chinese "super-embassy" in London, asserting the security threats raised "seem real" without making elaborations, according to the Times on local time Saturday.
  • HKSAR Security Secretary writes letter to refute unfounded smears related to Jimmy Lai in WSJ editorial Global Times 18 Jan 2026 -- Regarding the unfounded smear in an article published by The Wall Street Journal (WSJ) concerning the Jimmy Lai case, Chris Tang Ping-keung, secretary for security of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) government, has written a letter to the press to to express opposition against vicious attempts to interfere with Hong Kong's judicial system.
  • China's MSS discloses case of early involvement in construction planning to eliminate information leakage risks near sensitive facilities Global Times 18 Jan 2026 -- China's Ministry of State Security (MSS) on Sunday disclosed a construction project case in which the state security department eliminated security risks by revising planning for an urban village renovation project with significant information leakage concerns, effectively safeguarding a nearby sensitive facility.

  • DPP warns against 'cognitive warfare' over U.S. tariff deal CNA 18 Jan 2026 -- The ruling Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) urged the public on Sunday to remain vigilant against what it described as "cognitive warfare" relating to Taiwan's recent agreement with the United States to lower tariffs on Taiwanese goods to 15 percent.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 18 Jan 2026

  • Iranian Official Pledges 'Severest Punishments' Coming As New Details Of Crackdown Emerge RFE/RL 18 Jan 2026 -- New details of Iran's brutal crackdown on protests have continued to trickle out on January 18, as an Iranian judiciary official told reporters that "severest Islamic punishments" would be coming for those deemed "hostile."
  • Intelligence Ministry: 300 ringleaders of foreign-backed riots arrested across Iran Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Ministry of Intelligence says over 300 key operatives and ringleaders involved in the recent foreign-backed riots in the country have been arrested.
  • IRGC forces arrest riot ringleaders in Iran's Khuzestan, seize arms Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) forces have identified and arrested ringleaders and terrorist elements involved in recent foreign-linked riots in Iran's southwestern Khuzestan Province, confiscating various types of lethal weapons from them.
  • Iran begins easing bans on global internet Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- Iran has started restoring access to the global internet more than ten days after it was restricted in response to widespread unrest and acts of terrorism.
  • Any attack against Ayatollah Khamenei 'full-fledged' war with Iranian nation: President Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian warns that any attack against Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei will amount to a "full-fledged war" with the Iranian nation.
  • US, Israel suffered another defeat in terrorist riots after June war: Qalibaf Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf says the United States and Israeli regime suffered another defeat in the recent terrorist riots in Iran after the failure of their joint military aggression against the country in June last year.
  • Europe sidelining itself in global affairs by 'blindly following' US, Israel: Iran Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei says Europe is sidelining itself in global affairs by blindly following the United States and Israel in international developments.
  • Trump Says Time For 'New Leadership' in Iran Amid Deadly Protest Crackdown RFE/RL 18 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said it was time for new leaders to take control in Iran after tens of thousands of protesters have taken to the streets in the country, presenting the Islamic republic with one of the most serious threats to its legitimacy since the 1979 revolution.
  • US 'fueling tensions' in West Asia: Iran dismissed claim of planning attacks on American bases Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- Iran has rejected claims by the United States that it is planning attacks on American facilities, saying Washington is seeking to stoke tensions in West Asia.

  • US evacuation of Ain al-Asad base strengthens Iraq's national sovereignty: Araghchi Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the withdrawal of US military forces from Iraq's Ain al-Asad base will strengthen Iraq's national sovereignty, describing the move as a clear sign of growing independence and stability in the Arab country.
  • Iran Working on Security Agreement With Iraq - Foreign Minister Sputnik 18 Jan 2026 -- Iran is working with Iraq on a joint security agreement, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Sunday at a press briefing with Iraqi Foreign Minister Fuad Hussein, who arrived in Tehran for a visit.
  • President Pezeshkian vows historical Iran-Iraq relations will remain stable Press TV 18 Jan 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian has emphasized the deep-rooted ties between Iran and Iraq, characterizing the emotional and spiritual bonds between the two nations as the foundation of their long-standing relationship.

  • HRH the Crown Prince Receives Phone Call from Syrian President SPA 18 Jan 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, received a phone call today from President Ahmed Al-Sharaa of the Syrian Arab Republic

  • Prime Minister Carney secures new partnership with Qatar to increase trade, investment, and defence cooperation Prime Minister of Canada 18 Jan 2026
  • Prime Minister Carney meets with Amir of Qatar His Highness Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani Prime Minister of Canada 18 Jan 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, met with the Amir of Qatar, His Highness Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, in Doha, during the first-ever visit by a Canadian Prime Minister to Qatar.
  • Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada and Amir of Qatar, His Highness Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani commit to deepening bilateral engagement and economic cooperation Prime Minister of Canada 18 Jan 2026
